baseline.config_note: gqa_coop_vec sliced path, FLASH_L=32, 1 barrier per j-loop iteration (line 917), 128 WGs at ctx=512 (Hkv=8 * 16)

sources.barrier_count_note: 1 UOp.barrier() per NB=ceildiv(L=128, TK=16)=8 tile iterations (covers K+V staging together, line 1013). Sliced path: FLASH_L=128 inferred from 32 WGs = Hkv=8 * ceildiv(512, 128). Baseline FLASH_L=32 has 32 barriers (1 per j-loop position). G=5 has FEWER barriers than baseline.
sources.lds_bytes_note: 2 * TK=16 * Hd=128 * 2 bytes = 8192 (ksh + vsh, lines 977-978). Baseline stages K only: 1 * 128 * 2 = 256 bytes.
sources.work_per_wg_note: G=5: 5 Q-heads * 128 positions = 640 position-head pairs per WG. Baseline: 5 Q-heads * 32 positions = 160 per WG. 4x more work per WG.
sources.adjusted_slowdown: 2090 / (27 * 4) = 19.4x slower per unit of work (not 78x raw). Still ~20x too slow.
sources.wg_count_note: 32 WGs (G=5 sliced) vs 128 WGs (baseline). Both underoccupy 96-CU gfx1100.
sources.root_hypothesis: NOT barrier_flood (G=5 has fewer barriers). NOT layout_mismatch (WARPS=5=G is correct). Likely: LDS staging of BOTH K+V creates 32x more LDS traffic than baseline (K-only), or VGPR pressure from maintaining online softmax state across 5 GQA heads simultaneously.
